LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS SUPERSHUTTLE INTERNATION, INC. & TOM LEVOY'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT MODIFIED ITS RULING AS FOLLOWS: SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE SPECIFIC FACTS THAT ESTABLISH A BASIS OF DUTY THAT RUNS TO MR. ELAURIA FROM SUPER SHUTTLE INTERNATIONAL AND LAVOY THAT IS SEPARATE AND DISTINCT AND INDEPENDENT OF THE UNIFORM FRANCHISE AGREEMENT ("UFA"). THE COURT TAKES JUDICIAL NOTICE OF THE ARBITRATION AWARD BETWEEN PLAINTIFFS AND SUPER SHUTTLE SAN FRANCISCO ("SSSF"). THE ISSUE THERE WAS WHETHER SSSF BREACHED THE UFA RELATED TO OBTAINING UNINSURED MOTORIST COVERAGE, BREACHED A FIDUCIARY DUTY TO MR. ELAURIA OR DEFRAUDED MR. ELAURIA. IN DECIDING THE FRAUD CLAIM, THE ARBITRATOR ADDRESSED THE CONTENTION THAT SSSF FAILED TO PROVIDE REASONABLY NECESSARY INSURANCE AND THAT SSSF ALLEGEDLY PROFITED THROUGH ITS INSURANCE PROGRAM. THE ARBITRATOR RULED AGAINST MR. ELAURIA ON BOTH ISSUES AND ALSO FOUND THAT THERE WAS NO BREACH OF THE UFA AGREEMENT. PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT MAKES THE SAME CLAIMS AGAINST THESE DEFENDANTS - SSSF'S PARENT AND AN ALLEGED OWNER OF THE PARENT. THE CENTRAL CLAIM AGAINST THESE DEFENDANTS CENTERS ON OBTAINING VEHICLE INSURANCE, OVERCHARGING FRANCHISES FOR THE INSURANCE AND MISAPPROPRIATING THE FRANCHISES' INSURANCE PAYMENTS (PARAGRAPH 11). CENTRAL TO THE CLAIMS MADE IS THE CONTENTION THAT SECTION 5 OF THE UFA MADE FALSE REPRESENTATIONS AS TO INSURANCE COVERAGE (PARAGRAPHS 28-32). EACH OF PLAINTIFF'S CAUSES OF ACTION ARISES DIRECTLY THE ISSUES LITIGATED AND DECIDED IN THE PRIOR ARBITRATION. FOR THAT REASON THE ARBITRATION IS RES JUDICATA. MS. COLLIER TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-30-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS SUPERSHUTTLE INTERNATIONAL, INC AND TOM LEVOY'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT

Appearances:
Gregory Winslow of Winslow & Hurtubise (621-2131) for plaintiff and responding party
Sharon Cohen Collier of Archer Norris (925-930-6600) for defendants and moving parties

Argued and the Court modified its ruling as follows: sustained with 10 days leave to amend to allege specific facts that establish a basis of duty that runs to Mr. Elauria from Super Shuttle International and Lavoy that is separate and distinct and independent of the Uniform Franchise Agreement ("UFA"). The Court takes judicial notice of the arbitration award between plaintiffs and Super Shuttle San Francisco ("SSSF"). The issue there was whether SSSF breached the UFA related to obtaining uninsured motorist coverage, breached a fiduciary duty to Mr. Elauria or defrauded Mr. Elauria. In deciding the fraud claim, the arbitrator addressed the contention that SSSF failed to provide reasonably necessary insurance and that SSSF allegedly profited through its insurance program. The arbitrator ruled against Mr. Elauria on both issues and also found that there was no breach of the UFA agreement. Plaintiff's complaint makes the same claims against these defendants - SSSF's parent and an alleged owner of the parent. The central claim against these defendants centers on obtaining vehicle insurance, overcharging franchises for the insurance and misappropriating the franchises' insurance payments (paragraph 11). Central to the claims made is the contention that section 5 of the UFA made false representations as to insurance coverage (paragraphs 28-32). Each of plaintiff's causes of action arises directly the issues litigated and decided in the prior arbitration. For that reason the arbitration is res judicata. Ms. Collier to prepare a form of order. (302/PJM) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600
